zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Christine Joan Byrde於1492年出生在英國英格蘭素福爾可厄爾 斯頓哈姆時，她的父親Edward Byrde8歲，母親Sarah Appleton2歲。她於1530年與John Packard在英國英格蘭素福爾可厄爾 斯頓哈姆結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和6個女兒的父母。她去世於從1541年1月1日到1544年12月30日在她的家鄉，埋葬在英國英格蘭素福爾可厄爾 斯頓哈姆。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lady Christine Joan Byrde于1492年出生在英国英格兰素福爾可厄爾 斯頓哈姆时，她的父亲Edward Byrde8岁，母亲Sarah Appleton2岁。她于1530年与John Packard在英国英格兰素福爾可厄爾 斯頓哈姆结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和6个女儿的父母。她去世于从1541年1月1日到1544年12月30日在她的家乡，埋葬在英国英格兰素福爾可厄爾 斯頓哈姆。

nameMeanings.name: Byrde
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Bird
meanings: English and Scottish: nickname for a young or a small and slender person, from Middle English brid, bird, burd (Old English bird, brid, perhaps also byrd) ‘bird, young bird’, also ‘young man, young woman, child’.

Irish: Anglicized form of a number of Irish names erroneously thought to contain the element éan ‘bird’, in particular Ó hÉinigh (see Heagney ), Ó hÉanna (see Heaney ), Ó hÉanacháin (see Heneghan ), and Mac an Déaghanaigh (see McEneaney ).

Americanized form (translation into English) of various European surnames meaning ‘bird’, as for example German and Jewish (Ashkenazic) Vogel , French Loiseau , Czech Ptáček (see Ptacek ) and Pták, Polish Ptak .

nameMeanings.name: Christine
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of   Christina .  It was popular in the medieval period, when it appears to have been used interchangeably with   Christian  , and again in Britain at the end of the 19th century. In the United States it was particularly popular from the 1950s to the 1970s.

variants.name: Christeen
meanings: form of   Christina .  It was popular in the medieval period, when it appears to have been used interchangeably with   Christian  , and again in Britain at the end of the 19th century. In the United States it was particularly popular from the 1950s to the 1970s.

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Christine Joan Byrde was born in 1492, in Earl Stonham, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom, her father, Edward Byrde, was 8 and her mother, Sarah Appleton, was 2. She married John Packard in 1530, in Earl Stonham, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 6 daughters. She died from 1 January 1541 to 30 December 1544, in her hometown, and was buried in Earl Stonham, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lady Christine Joan Byrde est née en 1492, à Earl Stonham, Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Edward Byrde, avait 8 ans et sa mère, Sarah Appleton, avait 2 ans. Elle a épousé John Packard en 1530, à Earl Stonham, Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 6 filles. Elle est décédée du 1 janvier 1541 au 30 décembre 1544, dans sa ville natale, et a été enterrée à Earl Stonham, Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ja.Earl Stonham, Suffolk, England, United K: イギリスイギリススフォルクエアルル ストンハム
ja.lifeSummary: Lady Christine Joan Byrdeが1492年にイギリスイギリススフォルクエアルル ストンハムで生まれた時，父Edward Byrdeは8歳，母Sarah Appletonは2歳であった。1530年にイギリスイギリススフォルクエアルル ストンハムでJohn Packardと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と6人の娘の親であった。彼女はから1541年1月1日まで1544年12月30日に故郷で死去し，イギリスイギリススフォルクエアルル ストンハムにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Christine Joan Byrde(이)가 1492년 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 에를 스토함에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Edward Byrde(은)는 8세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Sarah Appleton(은)는 2세였다. 그녀는 1530년 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 에를 스토함에서 John Packard(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1541년 1월 1일부터 1544년 12월 30일까지 그녀의 고향에서 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 에를 스토함에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Christine Joan Byrde nasceu em 1492, em Earl Stonham, Suffolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Edward Byrde, tinha 8 anos e sua mãe, Sarah Appleton, tinha 2 anos. Ela casou-se com John Packard em 1530, em Earl Stonham, Suffolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 6 filhas. Ela faleceu de 1 de janeiro de 1541 a 30 de dezembro de 1544, em sua cidade natal, e foi sepultada em Earl Stonham, Suffolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady Christine Joan Byrde родилась в 1492 г. в местности эарл стонам, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Edward Byrde было 8 лет, а матери, которую звали Sarah Appleton, 2. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Packard в 1530 г. в местности эарл стонам, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 6 дочерей. Она умерла с 1 января 1541 по 30 декабря 1544 в её родном городе и была похоронена в местности эарл стонам, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Lady Christine Joan Byrde nació en 1492, en Earl Stonham, Suffolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Edward Byrde, tenía 8 años y su mamá, Sarah Appleton, tenía 2 años. Se casó con John Packard en 1530, en Earl Stonham, Suffolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 6 hijas. Murió desde 1 de enero de 1541 hasta 30 de diciembre de 1544, en su pueblo, y fue sepultada en Earl Stonham, Suffolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
